title: sideroxylon reclinatum subsp. rufotomentosum
intro: Sideroxylon reclinatum subsp. rufotomentosum, also known as the Rufous-barked Ironwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The Rufous-barked Ironwood has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, fever, and rheumatism, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of Sideroxylon reclinatum subsp. rufotomentosum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
